recipe.name: Eggplant and Minced Meat Pasta Casserole
ingredients: 18 oz (500g) ground pork and beef blend
1 head of cheese (such as mozzarella or mild cheddar), grated and divided
5 large white button mushrooms, finely chopped
3 teaspoons salt, divided (plus a pinch for pasta water)
1 small bunch scallions, finely chopped (for garnish)
instructions: Step 1 – Make the egg custard batter: Crack 4 eggs into a large bowl. Add 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon black pepper, and 1 teaspoon dried oregano. Whisk until combined. Pour in 1 cup heavy cream and 1 cup whole milk. Add 1/2 cup all-purpose flour and 1 tablespoon baking powder. Whisk until smooth and lump-free. Set aside.

Step 2 – Cook the pasta: Fill a saucepan with water, add a pinch of salt, and bring to a boil. Add 250g of pasta and cook according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and transfer to a large mixing bowl.

Step 3 – Cook the meat base: Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add the finely chopped white onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes. Add the grated garlic and cook for 1 minute. Add the 500g ground pork and beef blend and cook for 10–15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until browned and cooked through. Season with 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon black pepper, and 1 teaspoon dried oregano. Remove from heat.

Step 4 – Assemble the meat and pasta layer: Add the cooked ground meat mixture to the bowl with the drained pasta. Pour in the 200ml tomato sauce and stir to combine. Add half of the grated cheese and mix well. Grease a large baking dish with a little oil, then spread the pasta and meat mixture evenly across the bottom.

Step 5 – Cook the vegetable layer: Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Add the coarsely chopped red onion and sauté until soft, about 4 minutes. Add the eggplant strips and cook for 3 minutes. Add the chopped bell peppers and finely chopped mushrooms. Stir in the 400g canned diced tomatoes. Season with 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon black pepper, and 1 teaspoon dried oregano. Simmer for about 10 minutes until all vegetables are tender and well combined.

Step 6 – Layer and top the casserole: Transfer the cooked vegetable mixture on top of the pasta and meat layer in the baking dish, spreading it out evenly. Pour the egg custard batter evenly over the vegetables, ensuring it covers the entire surface.

Step 7 – Add cheese topping and bake: Sprinkle the remaining half of the grated cheese generously over the top. Preheat your oven to 360°F (180°C). Place the casserole in the oven and bake for 30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and bubbly.

Step 8 – Rest and garnish: Remove the casserole from the oven and allow it to cool slightly for 5–10 minutes. Score the top into approximately 12 equal portions. Place a halved cherry tomato on each portion and sprinkle finely chopped scallions over the top. Serve warm.

key_nutrients: High in protein from eggs, meat, and cheese
Good source of calcium from dairy and cheese
Rich in antioxidants from oregano, tomatoes, and bell peppers
Contains iron and B vitamins from ground meat
Provides complex carbohydrates from pasta and flour

recipe.storage: Allow the casserole to cool completely before storing. Cover tightly with plastic wrap or transfer individual portions to airtight containers. Refrigerate for up to 3–4 days. To freeze, wrap individual portions in plastic wrap and place in freezer-safe bags; freeze for up to 2 months. Reheat in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 15–20 minutes, or microwave individual portions on medium heat for 2–3 minutes until heated through.
recipe.serving_suggestions: Serve warm directly from the baking dish as a hearty main course. Pairs beautifully with a crisp green salad dressed with lemon vinaigrette, crusty artisan bread, or garlic bread on the side. A dollop of sour cream or plain Greek yogurt on top adds a lovely creamy contrast. For a lighter meal, serve alongside steamed or roasted vegetables. This dish is equally delicious the next day, making it an excellent choice for meal prep or packed lunches.
